# Supabase Remediation Checklist

**Scope:** reviewing, staging, testing, and eventually applying the Supabase client boundary split and `supabase/migrations/010_lockdown_anon_access.sql`.
**Status:** checklist, not a log. Check items off in a working branch; do not edit this file as a record of what was done.
**Related:** `docs/supabase-client-boundaries.md`, `docs/supabase-security-audit.md` (vault), `supabase/migrations/010_lockdown_anon_access.sql`.

---

## 1. Current Risk Summary

Audit findings (captured 2026-04-24, pre-migration state):

- `public.webhook_events` — RLS **off**; `anon` and `authenticated` hold INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE / TRUNCATE.
- `public.sync_runs` — RLS **off**; same broad DML grants.
- `public.organizations`, `public.digests` — RLS on with deny-all policies, but the underlying anon/authenticated DML role grants were never stripped; RLS is the sole defense on 4 of 6 public tables.
- `public.grants`, `public.funders` — intentionally anon-readable; the `anon_read_grants` / `anon_read_funders` SELECT policies are the correct shape.

Positive findings:

- No `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` outside `~/.claude/.env` (and `.env.local`, git-ignored).
- No Supabase Storage buckets configured.
- No Supabase Edge Functions deployed.
- Next.js app has been split into `supabase-server.ts` (service_role, `import "server-only"`) and `supabase-browser.ts` (anon key); legacy `src/lib/supabase.ts` deleted; `tsc --noEmit` passes.

**Residual risk:** migration 010 depends on the client boundary remaining true. Any client component that imports `supabase-server` ships the service-role key to end users and collapses every deny-all policy.

---

Client boundary split (already on disk, not yet committed):

Migration (drafted, **not executed**):

---

## 3. Pre-Commit Review

- [ ] Read `docs/supabase-client-boundaries.md` top-to-bottom; every row in the Validation table matches current `src/` state.
- [ ] Read `supabase/migrations/010_lockdown_anon_access.sql` top-to-bottom; all 5 blocks match the intent described in lines 4–22.
- [ ] Confirm no new files appear in `src/lib/` other than `supabase-server.ts` and `supabase-browser.ts`.
- [ ] Confirm no file contains both `SERVICE_ROLE` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`.
- [ ] Confirm `src/lib/supabase-server.ts` begins with `import "server-only";`.
- [ ] Confirm no `"use client"` file imports from `@/lib/supabase-server`.
- [ ] `git diff src/lib/` — every modified import updated to either `supabase-server` or `supabase-browser`, no dangling `@/lib/supabase` references.

---

## 4. Secret Scan

Run from repo root; each should exit with the expected pattern only, no unexpected matches:

- [ ] `rg -n 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' src docs scripts` — matches only `src/lib/supabase-server.ts`, `docs/supabase-client-boundaries.md`, `docs/supabase-remediation-checklist.md`, `docs/supabase-remediation-commit-plan.md`.
- [ ] `rg -n 'eyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}' src docs scripts` — no JWT string literals in tracked files.
- [ ] `rg -n 'sk_live_|sk_test_|whsec_' src docs scripts` — no Stripe key literals.
- [ ] `rg -n 'service_role' src` — only `src/lib/supabase-server.ts`.
- [ ] `grep -rn -E '[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\\.[a-z]{2,}' docs scripts | grep -v 'example.com\\|noreply\\|kaelen@\\|endersclarity@\\|@northstar'` — empty (no non-owner emails in docs or scripts).
- [ ] Check `.env.local` is git-ignored (`git check-ignore .env.local` returns nonzero exit = tracked; zero exit = ignored, expected).

---

## 5. Typecheck / Build Validation

- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` — exit 0.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` — lint delta is zero; any pre-existing error count must match the count on `main` (recorded in `docs/supabase-client-boundaries.md` row 6: 19 pre-existing).
- [ ] `pnpm build` — succeeds locally. Do not push build artifacts.

---

## 6. Client / Server Import Boundary Validation

Run `scripts/check_supabase_boundaries.sh` (see `docs/supabase-client-boundaries.md` for the canonical table).

- [ ] `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` references appear only in `src/lib/supabase-server.ts`.
- [ ] `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` references appear only in `src/lib/supabase-browser.ts`.
- [ ] No `"use client"` file imports `@/lib/supabase-server` (grep head-of-file `"use client"` then scan for the import).
- [ ] No legacy `@/lib/supabase` or `./supabase` imports remain.
- [ ] No non-server file reads `process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.
- [ ] `src/lib/supabase-server.ts` line 1 is exactly `import "server-only";`.

Script must exit nonzero on any violation.

---

## 7. Commit / Staging Plan

See `docs/supabase-remediation-commit-plan.md` for the exact two-commit sequence.

- [ ] Branch off `main`: `git checkout -b chore/supabase-lockdown`.
- [ ] Commit 1: client boundary split + docs + boundary-check script.
- [ ] Commit 2: migration 010 (separate commit, can be reverted without reverting the boundary split).
- [ ] Push branch (not main) after both commits verify.
- [ ] Open PR; do not auto-merge; require explicit approval before merge.
- [ ] Do not tag, do not release, do not deploy until sections 8–15 are checked.

---

## 8. Preview Deployment Checklist

- [ ] Vercel preview deploys on PR push; confirm build passes in Vercel.
- [ ] Preview env vars: confirm preview uses the same `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` but a separate Supabase project or a read-only role if available.
- [ ] Preview does not run migrations; Vercel has no Supabase migration step in CI.
- [ ] Visit preview URL, confirm `/grants` renders (anon SELECT still works), `/settings` redirects to sign-in flow cleanly, `/unsubscribe` handles a test token.
- [ ] Preview network tab: no `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` appears in any response body or client bundle. Search preview `_next/static/chunks/*.js` for the literal string `service_role` — expect zero matches.

---

## 9. Smoke Tests Before Migration

All run against the **pre-010** state so we know current behavior, to compare post-apply.

- [ ] `/api/sync-grants` returns 200 and records a row in `sync_runs`.
- [ ] `/api/send-digests` dry-run returns 200.
- [ ] `/api/webhooks/stripe` test event processes without error and inserts to `webhook_events`.
- [ ] `/grants` page renders grants list for signed-out visitor (anon SELECT on `grants`).
- [ ] `/settings` page loads authenticated content via `/api/settings` (service_role under the hood; no direct anon access to `organizations`).
- [ ] Capture row counts for `grants`, `funders`, `organizations`, `sync_runs`, `webhook_events`, `digests`. Record them in a scratchpad (not committed). These are sanity checks, not backups.

---

## 10. Migration Staging Checklist

**Hard gate:** do not proceed to this section until sections 1–9 are clean.

- [ ] Confirm migration 010 exists on the branch: `git show HEAD:supabase/migrations/010_lockdown_anon_access.sql | head -40`.
- [ ] Confirm no earlier migration in the `supabase/migrations/` folder conflicts (009 applied upstream already; 010 is additive).
- [ ] Choose staging target: Supabase **staging project** (preferred) OR Supabase **production project** during a low-traffic window. Never apply to production as the first run.
- [ ] If no staging project exists: create a branch via Supabase branches (`list_branches` first to confirm none exist). Do not apply 010 to `main` until it has been exercised on a branch.
- [ ] Dry-run the migration SQL with `BEGIN; ... ROLLBACK;` wrapping; the file's own transaction block makes this straightforward. Confirm no errors.
- [ ] Apply 010 on the branch. Keep the apply session open; be ready to run the rollback block if V1–V4 fail.
- [ ] **Do not apply via `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__apply_migration` without explicit user approval in the session.** This file is a checklist, not an authorization.

---

## 11. Verification Queries After Migration

Run the queries defined in lines 126–165 of `010_lockdown_anon_access.sql`. Expected results:

- [ ] **V1 — RLS status.** All 6 public tables show `rowsecurity = true`.
- [ ] **V2 — Advisor.** `get_advisors(project_id, type="security")` returns no `rls_disabled_in_public` findings.
- [ ] **V3 — Policies.** `sync_runs` and `webhook_events` each have 4 deny-all policies (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) with `qual='false'` or `with_check='false'`.
- [ ] **V4 — Role grants.** `anon` and `authenticated` have:
  - SELECT only on `grants`, `funders`.
  - No privileges on `organizations`, `digests`, `sync_runs`, `webhook_events`.

If any V-query fails, follow section 13 rollback.

---

## 12. App Smoke Tests After Migration

Compare against the pre-migration smoke tests in section 9.

- [ ] `/api/sync-grants` still returns 200; `sync_runs` row inserted (service_role bypass works).
- [ ] `/api/send-digests` still 200.
- [ ] `/api/webhooks/stripe` test event still processes; `webhook_events` insert succeeds.
- [ ] `/grants` page still renders (anon SELECT preserved).
- [ ] `/settings` still works end-to-end.
- [ ] Check Vercel function logs for any `permission denied for table` errors in the 30 minutes after apply.
- [ ] Re-check row counts from section 9 — should match exactly (no unintended writes or deletes during migration).

---

## 13. Rollback Procedure

If V1–V4 fail, or if app smoke tests surface permission errors, execute the rollback block from lines 94–124 of `010_lockdown_anon_access.sql`:

- [ ] Run the rollback SQL (uncommented) in a transaction on the same target.
- [ ] Re-run V1 — expect `rowsecurity = false` on `sync_runs`, `webhook_events`.
- [ ] Re-run smoke tests from section 9 to confirm restoration.
- [ ] Revert the migration commit on the branch if the rollback was applied to production (`git revert <commit>`); leave the boundary-split commit in place.
- [ ] File an incident note in `docs/DEVLOG.md` with the V-query output that triggered rollback.

**Rollback does not remove the commit from git history** — that requires a follow-up revert PR. Do not `git push --force`.

---

## 14. Conditions That Should Block Execution

Stop and escalate if any of these are true:

- A `"use client"` file imports from `@/lib/supabase-server`.
- `rg 'service_role' src` returns matches outside `src/lib/supabase-server.ts`.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` appears in any server-only file (dual-role client reintroduced).
- `tsc --noEmit` fails.
- Lint error count regresses against `main`.
- A new public table exists without RLS + policies.
- Supabase Auth has been enabled since audit (check via `list_extensions` for `supabase_auth` usage).
- Supabase Storage buckets now exist (check via advisor).
- Any Edge Function now exists (check via `list_edge_functions`).
- Non-owner emails present in `docs/` or `scripts/` files being committed.
- `.env.local` is staged.
- This checklist's section 4 secret scan finds any unexpected match.

---

## 15. Follow-Up Audit After Execution

Schedule these for the week after apply:

- [ ] Re-run vault `docs/supabase-security-audit.md` inventory scripts; confirm `supabase-security-inventory.json` reflects RLS=true and no broad DML grants.
- [ ] Run Supabase advisor again: `get_advisors(project_id, type="performance")` — confirm no new findings (RLS policies can add plan cost).
- [ ] Tag migration 010 commit with a note in `docs/DEVLOG.md` describing apply date, operator, and verification output.
- [ ] Add a CI step (future work) that runs `scripts/check_supabase_boundaries.sh` on every PR touching `src/lib/` or `supabase/migrations/`.
- [ ] Revisit `authenticated` role grants if Supabase Auth is added; current lockdown assumes auth is unused.
- [ ] Confirm no follow-up migrations were added that reintroduce default grants; Supabase historically re-applies defaults during maintenance.

---

## Usage Notes for `scripts/check_supabase_boundaries.sh`

- Read-only; never writes to Supabase.
- Redacts any matched secret value in output (prints file:line, not the literal).
- Exits nonzero on boundary violations so it can be wired into CI or a pre-commit hook later.
- Does not execute migrations or query Supabase. The "010 drafted but not executed" state cannot be proven locally — the script notes this explicitly.
- Optional: prints the command to run `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` if it detects tsc is installed; it will not run it automatically.

Invocation:

```
bash scripts/check_supabase_boundaries.sh
```

Exit codes:

- `0` — all checks pass.
- `1` — one or more boundary violations.
- `2` — script misconfiguration (missing `rg`, missing repo root).
